body.title: DS ToolLoop: OR models never return DONE: prefix — prompt forces correct exit token
body.result: superseded by T-ds-cost-cap which adds cost cap framing
body.intention: I intend that DS completes S-size tickets at haiku tier without cascading to opus due to missing DONE: exit token.
body.description:
OR models (haiku/sonnet/opus) complete work correctly but return bare analysis text instead of DONE: prefix. This triggers the DONE: gate in _post_result, escalates to CC, and Granny re-dispatches — causing the full haiku→sonnet→opus cascade to run repeatedly on a single ticket (burned ~$80 on 2026-06-11).

Root cause: SYSTEM_PROMPT in device.py and SYSTEM_RULES in toolloop.py mention DONE: as a completion format but bury it. OR models respond to each turn independently and don't enforce the exit token. Fix: make DONE: the *only* valid non-tool response format — any prose without it is a model output bug, not a continuation.

Second fix: tier cascade in _run_inference() should not advance to sonnet/opus when haiku actually executed tool calls (MAX_TURNS hit after real work = capability/complexity issue, not a tier miss). Only advance on zero-tool-call response or explicit ESCALATE:.

**Affected files:** devices/dicksimnel/device.py (SYSTEM_PROMPT, _run_inference), devices/dicksimnel/toolloop.py (SYSTEM_RULES)
**Design rules:** no-sqlite, test-plan-or-why-not
**Scope boundary:** In: SYSTEM_PROMPT rewrite to enforce DONE:, cascade guard on tool-call count. Out: new OR models, ToolLoop architecture changes.
**Completion criteria:** Run DS on a trivial ticket (T-spreading-activation-test-stale-path or similar S-size); observe haiku returns DONE: on first successful run without cascade to sonnet/opus. Log shows no 'result missing DONE: prefix' warning.
